name: Enterprise Architect (Agent User)
description: Primary user who reviews agent proposals and manages architecture truth

name: CIO / CTO (Agent Sponsor)
description: Executive sponsor who needs audit-proof provenance and governance over AI agents

name: Security Officer (Agent Governance)
description: Ensures agents operate within RBAC boundaries and no unauthorized graph writes occur

name: Agent Developer
description: Developer who builds and configures MCP-compatible discovery agents for TheArchitect

name: CMDB Data Gap
description: Enterprise architects spend 60-80% of time on manual data collection instead of analysis

name: Manual Maintenance Cost
description: Quarterly review cycles cause architecture models to be outdated within weeks of creation

name: Agent Proliferation Risk
description: Uncontrolled AI agents accessing enterprise systems create shadow-agent governance gaps

name: Agentic AI Market Momentum
description: OpenClaw and NemoClaw ecosystems growing rapidly - first-mover advantage window is 12-18 months

name: Current Manual Process
description: Existing data maintenance is interview-based and Excel-driven with quarterly update cycles

name: Agent Security Landscape
description: OpenClaw agents are highly permissive by default - enterprise governance requires deny-by-default sandboxing

name: No Competitor Offers Agent-EAM
description: LeanIX, Ardoq, Mega HOPEX have no autonomous agent integration for architecture graph maintenance

name: 80% Maintenance Reduction
description: Reduce manual architecture data collection effort by 80% through autonomous reader agents

name: Continuous Graph Freshness
description: Architecture graph updated continuously instead of quarterly - single source of truth guaranteed

name: Full Provenance Tracking
description: Every element traceable to its data source with confidence score and verification timestamp

name: Agent Governance Compliance
description: All agent activities auditable, RBAC-enforced, and approvable through human-in-the-loop workflow

name: Real-time Architecture Truth
description: Architecture graph reflects actual IT landscape state within hours instead of months

name: Audit-ready Data Provenance
description: Regulators and auditors can verify source and confidence of every architecture element

name: Proactive Agent Anomaly Detection
description: Advisor detects agent batch anomalies and scope violations before they corrupt the graph

name: Investor-demonstrable Auto-Discovery
description: 60-second demo showing autonomous graph population for Pre-Seed pitch

name: Architecture Wahrheit
description: The graph becomes the single source of truth because agents keep it perpetually current

name: Governance ohne Overhead
description: Human-in-the-loop approval with auto-approve for high-confidence items - control without bottleneck

name: REQ-MCP-Compatibility
description: Agent connector must implement Model Context Protocol for OpenClaw/NemoClaw compatibility

name: REQ-RBAC-Agent-Tokens
description: Agent authentication via JWT tokens with granular permissions: agent:read, agent:suggest, agent:write

name: REQ-Fuzzy-Match
description: Reconciliation must match elements by name similarity (Jaro-Winkler >= 0.85) plus type and layer

name: REQ-Schema-Validation
description: All agent-submitted data must pass Zod schema validation and injection-pattern detection

name: REQ-Human-Approval
description: No agent-proposed change reaches the graph without human approval or auto-approve threshold >= 0.9

name: REQ-Provenance-Fields
description: Every element must track lastUpdatedBy, lastUpdatedSource, confidenceScore, and lastVerifiedAt

name: REQ-Audit-Trail
description: Every agent discovery call and approval action must be logged in the existing audit system

name: REQ-Auto-Approve-No-Delete
description: Auto-approve must never be enabled for delete operations or status changes to retired

name: CONS-No-Autonomous-Write
description: Phase 1 agents are strictly read-suggest - no direct graph writes without approval workflow

name: CONS-Existing-Stack
description: Implementation must use existing Neo4j, MongoDB, Express, React stack - no new infrastructure

name: CONS-Solo-Developer
description: 6-week implementation window with single developer - scope must be ruthlessly prioritized

name: Reader-Before-Actor
description: Reader agents must build and validate the graph before any actor agent is allowed to execute changes

name: Graph-as-Agent-Memory
description: The Neo4j graph serves as the single contextual memory for all agents - no external knowledge bases

name: Agent Discovery Capability
description: Capability to receive and process architecture element proposals from external AI agents

name: Graph Reconciliation Capability
description: Capability to diff agent proposals against existing graph and produce merge suggestions

name: Approval Governance Capability
description: Capability to review, approve, reject, or edit agent proposals before graph application

name: Agent Telemetry Capability
description: Capability to monitor agent activity, approval rates, confidence scores, and graph freshness

name: Source Provenance Capability
description: Capability to track and display the origin, confidence, and verification state of every element

name: Implement MCP Agent Connector
description: Build abstraction layer with MCP as first implementation for agent-to-graph communication

name: Build Reconciliation Engine
description: Develop fuzzy-match and schema-validation pipeline that produces reviewable proposals

name: Add Approval Workflow
description: Create UI panel and API for human review of agent proposals with batch operations

name: Extend Advisor with Agent Detectors
description: Add detectors #12 (stale elements) and #13 (agent anomalies) to existing advisor engine

name: Build Demo Agent for Pitch
description: Create self-architecture discovery agent that demonstrates the full flow in 60 seconds

name: Agent-to-Graph Value Stream
description: End-to-end flow from agent discovery through reconciliation and approval to graph update

name: MCP SDK Resource
description: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk npm package for MCP server implementation

name: LLM API Budget (Agent)
description: Anthropic/OpenAI API budget for demo agent LLM calls and description enrichment

name: Agent Operator (Role)
description: Role: configures agent tokens, monitors agent dashboard, manages auto-approve thresholds

name: Proposal Reviewer (Role)
description: Role: reviews and approves/rejects agent-submitted reconciliation proposals

name: Submit Discovery Batch
description: Process: agent authenticates via JWT, submits AgentDiscoveryBatch to agent API endpoint

name: Reconcile Against Graph
description: Process: fuzzy-match elements against Neo4j, schema-validate, produce ReconciliationProposal

name: Review Agent Proposal
description: Process: EA opens Approval Panel, reviews diff for each item, approves/rejects/edits

name: Auto-Approve High Confidence
description: Process: items with confidence >= threshold and allowed action types are auto-approved to graph

name: Apply Approved Changes
description: Process: approved items are written to Neo4j in a single transaction with provenance metadata

name: Generate Agent Token
description: Process: admin generates JWT with agent:read/suggest/write permissions scoped to project

name: Monitor Agent Activity
description: Process: review agent dashboard for approval rates, anomalies, and graph freshness metrics

name: Agent Discovery Service (Business)
description: Service: accepts agent discovery batches and orchestrates reconciliation pipeline

name: Approval Workflow Service (Business)
description: Service: manages proposal lifecycle from pending through review to approved/rejected

name: Agent Telemetry Service (Business)
description: Service: aggregates and presents agent activity metrics for governance reporting

name: Discovery Batch Received
description: Event: triggered when an agent submits a new discovery batch via MCP or REST

name: Proposal Ready for Review
description: Event: triggered when reconciliation produces a proposal with items requiring human review

name: Graph Updated by Agent
description: Event: triggered when approved agent items are successfully written to Neo4j

name: Agent Anomaly Detected
description: Event: triggered when Advisor detects batch-size anomaly or scope violation from an agent

name: Agent Discovery Report
description: Object: summary of agent discovery activity including elements found, matched, and proposed

name: AgentDiscoveryBatch
description: Entity: container with agentId, timestamp, discovered elements array, discovered connections array, sourceSystem

name: DiscoveredElement
description: Entity: agent-proposed element with name, type, layer, status, confidence (0-1), rawMetadata, evidence

name: DiscoveredConnection
description: Entity: agent-proposed connection with sourceName, targetName, type, confidence, evidence text

name: ReconciliationProposal
description: Entity: MongoDB document with items array, status (pending/approved/rejected/partial), reviewedBy

name: ReconciliationItem
description: Entity: single diff item with action (create/update/flag_conflict/flag_stale), proposed vs existing, autoApprovable flag

name: AgentToken
description: Entity: JWT token document with agentId, projectId, permissions array, expiry, created metadata

name: ElementProvenance
description: Entity: provenance extension on ArchitectureElement - lastUpdatedBy, lastUpdatedSource, confidenceScore, lastVerifiedAt

name: AgentTelemetryRecord
description: Entity: aggregated metrics per agent - proposals count, approval rate, avg confidence, last active

name: Agent Reconciliation Data Model
description: Model: MongoDB schemas for ReconciliationProposal and AgentToken with TTL and compound indexes

name: MCP Connector
description: Component: MCP server implementation registering submit_discovery_batch and get_project_elements tools

name: Reconciliation Engine
description: Component: fuzzy-match algorithm (Jaro-Winkler) plus schema validator producing ReconciliationProposals

name: Schema Validator
description: Component: Zod-based validation with injection detection - blocks Cypher injection, HTML, overlength strings

name: Auto-Approve Engine
description: Component: evaluates items against configurable threshold and action whitelist for automatic approval

name: Graph Update Service
description: Component: applies approved items to Neo4j in atomic transaction with provenance metadata and WebSocket emit

name: Agent Auth Middleware
description: Component: JWT verification for agent tokens with permission checking (agent:read/suggest/write)

name: Demo Discovery Agent
description: Component: Node.js script acting as MCP client that discovers TheArchitect self-architecture

name: GitHub Discovery Agent
description: Component: stretch-goal agent parsing package.json and service files to extract ArchiMate elements

name: Advisor Detector Stale Elements
description: Component: Advisor detector #12 - flags elements with confidenceScore < 0.5 or lastVerified > 90 days

name: Advisor Detector Agent Anomalies
description: Component: Advisor detector #13 - flags batch-size anomalies (>50 items) and scope violations

name: Approval Panel
description: Component: React sidebar tab showing pending proposals with diff view, batch actions, and edit-and-approve

name: Provenance Badge
description: Component: UI element in PropertyPanel showing source, confidence bar, and last-verified date

name: Agent Dashboard
description: Component: React dashboard tab showing per-agent metrics, approval rates, and graph freshness score

name: Agent Token Manager
description: Component: UI in Project Settings for generating and revoking agent JWT tokens with permission selection

name: Agent REST API
description: Service: Express routes at /api/projects/:id/agent for discovery, proposals, approvals, and telemetry

name: Agent WebSocket Events
description: Service: Socket.IO events for real-time proposal notifications and graph-update broadcasts

name: MCP Tool Interface
description: Interface: MCP tool definitions - submit_discovery_batch, get_project_elements, get_element_schema

name: Agent REST Interface
description: Interface: REST endpoints - POST /discovery, GET /proposals, POST /approve, GET /telemetry

name: MCP SDK (@modelcontextprotocol/sdk)
description: npm package providing MCP server and client protocol implementation

name: string-similarity (npm)
description: npm package for Jaro-Winkler and Levenshtein string distance calculations in fuzzy matching

name: Neo4j Provenance Extension
description: Artifact: Cypher migration script adding lastUpdatedBy, lastUpdatedSource, confidenceScore, lastVerifiedAt to ArchitectureElement nodes

name: MongoDB Agent Collections
description: Artifact: Mongoose schemas for ReconciliationProposal (TTL 30d) and AgentToken with compound indexes

name: Agent Token JWT Secret
description: Artifact: separate JWT signing secret for agent tokens stored in .env (AGENT_JWT_SECRET)
